MASTER CALENDAR COURT

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/SKF)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CAMERON TURNER's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

Off calendar as untimely per CCP 1005(b).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-31-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Before the court is a Motion to be Relieved as Counsel from Counsel Gregory Kenyota and Bochner PLLC, counsel of record for Defendant Cameron C. Turner. The motion is OFF CALENDAR.

Moving Parties' motion was neither timely filed nor timely served. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b); Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail to place outside State adds ten court days to "any period of notice"].) see also Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Thus, the motion is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-25-2025 Continued To Jan-12-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jun-25-2025 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar May-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: (via Zoom videoconference).
Atty. Alana S. Yakovlev, ESQ. Bochner PLLC. (Tel:646-971-0685) for defendant Cameron Turner.
No other appearances made in this matter.

The court did not receive a contested email regarding this matter. It has informed the Counsel of this issue, , and the case will not be heard.

DEFENDANT CAMERON TURNER's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

Party Cameron C. Turner counsel Alana S. Yakolev and Bochner PLLC's motion to be relieved as counsel is DENIED.

The departure of an attorney from a firm is not necessarily grounds for the firm to withdraw as counsel and the record does not establish grounds for a court-ordered withdrawal at this time. In any event, the court will not authorize a withdrawal less than six weeks before trial. Counsel must first obtain a trial continuance (which is not available from this department). Further if the client is served at a last known address, section 3(b) of the declaration must be completed.

Order Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
